title: Chalet Hotels eyes Jaipur, Jodhpur and Hyderabad as future growth clusters
summary: Chalet Hotels Limited, backed by K Raheja Corp, is focusing on expanding into leisure markets and evaluating opportunities in Hyderabad. The company continues to grow its portfolio and maintain strong revenue growth despite challenges in core city markets, driven by a robust demand-supply dynamic.

title: Singapore Airlines says Pakistan's airspace closure hurt Air India more than foreign airlines
summary: Singapore Airlines CEO Goh Choon Phong highlighted that Air India's inability to use Pakistani airspace puts it at a disadvantage compared to foreign carriers. The closure has impacted Air India's operational costs and contributed to its mounting losses amidst broader industry challenges.

title: Apple anti-trust probe: Delhi HC asks CCI to hold its order till 15 July, orders firm to cooperate
summary: The court refused to grant Apple any further extension for appearing before the CCI and furnishing information sought by the regulator regarding its domestic turnover

title: Rapido raises $240 million led by Prosus at $3 billion valuation
summary: Rapido will use the fresh capital to deepen expansion beyond metros, strengthen supply and invest in technology and drivers.

title: US SEC’s settlement with Gautam Adani does not end Justice Department’s criminal investigation
summary: The SEC's civil case settlement against the Adani family does not shield them from criminal liability in a separate DoJ investigation. The Adanis face significant fines while denying allegations of bribery and securities fraud linked to solar-energy contracts.

title: How rising crude oil prices and market volatility could impact your personal finances
summary: Stock market volatility driven by rising crude oil prices, FII sell-offs, and inflation concerns reshapes the personal finance outlook, underscoring the importance of disciplined SIP investing, diversification, and long-term wealth creation.&nbsp;

title: What the US CLARITY Act could mean for India’s crypto future – Expert says, ‘rules must fit India’s large market’
summary: The Senate Banking Committee advanced the CLARITY Act, aiming to regulate cryptocurrencies amid industry tensions. Supported by some Democrats, the bill moves to the full Senate, indicating a shift towards structured crypto policymaking and potential global regulatory implications.

title: Trump-Xi summit: the 3 big takeaways from historic meeting in Beijing
summary: The two-day meeting wrapped up Friday, setting the tone for further U.S.-China talks this year.

title: Stock Radar: Narayana Hrudayalaya forms inverse Head & Shoulders pattern; where is the stock headed?
summary: Narayana Hrudayalaya Ltd has formed a bullish inverse head and shoulders pattern on its daily charts, signaling a potential trend reversal. Experts suggest short-term traders with a high-risk profile can consider buying the stock for a target above 2,000 levels in the next 1-2 months.

title: Nifty to hit 42,000 by 2028? New paper that studied market cycles says it's possible if these factors align
summary: A CNI InfoXchange study projects the Nifty could surpass 42,000 by 2028 if sustained foreign investor inflows return. The report highlights stronger domestic liquidity, resilient equity markets, robust GDP growth prospects, rising retail participation, and India’s increasing weight in global emerging market indices.

title: Hindustan Copper Q4 results 2026: Profit jumps 137% YoY to  ₹444 crore; board recommends final dividend of  ₹1.86 for FY26
summary: Hindustan Copper Q4 results 2026: Consolidated profit jumped 137% YoY to  ₹444 crore. On a standalone basis, the company's profit came at  ₹444.06 crore, up 133% YoY.

title: ITC Hotels Q4 Results: Cons PAT jumps 23% YoY to  ₹316 crore; Re 1 dividend announced
summary: ITC Hotels reported March quarter revenue from operations at  ₹1,253.7 crore, up 18.2% YoY. The hotels segment saw a slight decline of 2.5%, while the real estate segment grew significantly by 60% to  ₹130 crore.

title: Expert View | Markets are unlikely to see a runaway rally in 2026: Inderbir Singh Jolly, CEO, PL Private Wealth
summary: From an asset allocation perspective, we continue to maintain a balanced and diversified approach across equities, fixed income and strategic alternatives, said Inderbir Singh Jolly, CEO, PL Private Wealth.

title: Top Gainers & Losers on May 15: HUDCO, Muthoot Finance, Voltas, IOCL, Vedanta, HAL, Aditya Infotech among top losers
summary: On May 15, Indian markets declined, with Nifty down 0.18% and Sensex down 0.25%. Selling in financial and metal stocks offset IT sector gains. The rupee hit a record low of 96.5 against the dollar, influenced by foreign investor withdrawals and rising crude oil prices.

title: Sensex falls 161 points, Nifty 50 ends at 23,643; investors lose more than  ₹2 lakh crore in a day
summary: Stock market today: The Sensex fell 161 points, or 0.21%, to end at 75,237.99, while the Nifty 50 settled at 23,643.50, down 46 points, or 0.19%.

title: Shakti Press rights issue date extended. Size, allotment date, other details
summary: Shakti Press Ltd has extended its rights issue closing date to May 29, 2026, from May 18, 2026, to give shareholders more time to participate. The extension was approved during a May 11 meeting, prompting a nearly 5% stock increase.

title: Goldline Pharmaceutical IPO allotment date in focus today; latest GMP, step-by-step guide to check status
summary: Goldline Pharmaceutical IPO allotment finalisation occurs today, 15 May. Investors can check their allocation status on Bigshare Services Pvt Ltd. The IPO was oversubscribed 840.74 times, and the listing is set for 19 May, with shares credited to demat accounts on 18 May.

title: Tata Cliq leans on premium brands to stand out in e-commerce race
summary: Ten years since it first launched, Tata Cliq has exited some categories like electronics as it sharpens focus on premium brands like American athleisure brand Lululemon and luxury categories such as watches

title: Supreme Court gives Flipkart eight weeks to clear MarQ inventory in trademark dispute
summary: The top court refused to interfere with lower court orders, and held that Flipkart’s MarQ branding was deceptively similar to Marc Enterprises’ trademark.

title: Domestic travellers can now buy annual travel insurance for frequent trips: What's included, how it works and more
summary: The domestic travel insurance policy provides protection against several travel-specific risks and crises, including a personal injury benefit reaching  ₹3 lakh and medical facility coverage up to  ₹1 lakh during transit.

title: EPFO plans auto-settlement for final PF withdrawals. How will it benefit its 7 crore members?
summary: The EPFO auto-settlement facility aims to accelerate final PF withdrawals, reduce paperwork and streamline account transfers through a fully digital system for its more than seven crore members.

title: UPI's per-day and per-transaction limit set at  ₹2 lakh in Europe. What it means for users
summary: The per-transaction and per-day limit for UPI Global Acceptance in Europe (peer-to-merchant) has now been increased to  ₹2 lakh. Each member bank may set its own norms, subject to the overall applicable limits, NPCI has said.

title: Did you receive money in your bank account by mistake? Follow these steps to avoid tax liability
summary: Unexpected bank credits can become taxable unexplained income; follow prompt steps—do not use funds, notify the bank and sender, document everything, and consult a tax professional to avoid tax liability.&nbsp;

title: What happens to your EPF account if you become an NRI? Rules explained
summary: The EPF account remains active when an Indian becomes an NRI, but rules for contributions and withdrawals change. NRIs must meet eligibility conditions to withdraw funds online. Details here.

title: Bill Ackman built Microsoft stake in first quarter during selloff, betting on AI and cloud growth
summary: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square Capital Management has built a position in Microsoft, the billionaire hedge fund manager said Friday in a post on X.

title: Sebi expands permitted use of borrowings for highly leveraged InvITs
summary: The changes, which have come into force with immediate effect, are aimed at providing greater flexibility to InvITs in managing funding requirements.

title: Gainers & Losers: HAL, HUDCO among 6 top stock movers on Friday
Tata Steel Q4 Results: Profit zooms 147% YoY to  ₹2,965 crore; dividend of  ₹4 announced
summary: Tata Steel Q4 Results: The profit after tax (PAT) stood at  ₹2,965 crore as against  ₹1,200.88 crore in the same period a year ago.

title: Small-cap stock Standard Engineering edges higher despite stock market sell-off
summary: On May 15, Standard Engineering Technology stock rose 1.75% to  ₹136.60, reflecting strong Q4FY26 and FY26 performance. Total income surged 34.97% YoY, with notable profit growth, despite recent declines in share prices due to heavy selling pressure since September 2025.

title: JioStar initiates legal measures against Zee entertainment for alleged unauthorised airing of Bollywood films: Report
summary: JioStar, backed by Reliance and Disney, has reportedly sued Zee Entertainment, alleging the broadcaster aired Bollywood films without authorisation. This action follows Zee's earlier reported lawsuit claiming JioStar used its music without permission.&nbsp;

title: Vertex Ventures eyes semiconductors, robotics and batteries in deeptech pivot
summary: The firm is shifting its focus toward ‘sovereign technology’ as it nears full deployment of its $540-million fourth fund, managing partner Ben Mathias told Mint.

title: Gift tax rules explained: Who can give you money or assets without tax implications?
summary: Gifts exceeding  ₹50,000 are taxable under the Income Tax Act as income from other sources. Exemptions apply for gifts from relatives or on special occasions. Full list of exemptions here.

title: You can earn up to  ₹69,000 monthly income from  ₹1 crore FD in small finance banks. Here's how
summary: Small finance bank FDs can help deliver attractive monthly income on  ₹1 crore deposits, with senior citizens earning higher returns through competitive fixed deposit interest rates.

title: Buying or selling property in India as an NRI? Here are the rules you should know
summary: Non-Resident Indians can buy property in India under FEMA and RBI regulations. They may purchase residential and commercial properties, but agricultural land is subject to restrictions.&nbsp;
